_meta.purpose: Blind ground-truth labels for the drawe golden-set. Authored by viewing each image directly BEFORE reading any system/axis output, to avoid anchoring to current behavior.
_meta.clarity: clear = a competent artist would agree on the primary issue; ambiguous = the input is intrinsically unstable (stylized, conflicting cues, or the 'defect' is a deliberate style choice). Accuracy is scored on clear set only; ambiguous set is scored by abstain / weak-grade ratio.
_meta.fields: track=expected routing; expected_primary=most teachable single issue (artist judgment); acceptable=axes that would also be a defensible primary; for hands view/structure/foreshort = expected VLM read.

intended_tracks._note: Production-faithful path a real user/UI provides. null = auto-detection IS the best path (hands/landscapes route correctly auto, and auto adds the hand_structure intent term that explicit track='hand' drops — a known asymmetry). Non-null ONLY for figures, where a STYLE hint (real/anime/chibi) adds info auto-detect can't reliably get. face/edge → null (no face profile = E gap; no-subject).

images.notes: V-sign, clean line art, back of hand. Flat/line — structure read should NOT be 입체.
Palm facing, 5 fingers spread, colored/shaded -> 입체 ok.
Elongated fingers with skeletal underdrawing — proportion is the teachable issue.
Hand pointing at viewer, strong foreshortening + crosshatch. View is genuinely hard to call — VLM view flips expected; acceptable to abstain on view but structure=입체.
Palm-up at angle, intended for a ratio/proportion message. Should respond to proportion ask.
Blank paper. MUST NOT confabulate an axis — expect refuse/clarify/no-subject.
Dense scribble, no readable subject. Expect clarify/abstain, not a confident axis.
Image is a hand but message says face. Routing-conflict test: should either follow image (hand) or clarify, NOT hallucinate face feedback.
Front head/shoulders, pencil, light shading, normal proportions. No full-body pose -> pose should degrade gracefully.
Profile, pure outline (no shading) -> line/edge focus, not value.
3/4 anime portrait, colored. Color/value axes plausible since it's painted.
Full anatomical male, front, normal. Pose should extract cleanly. 'normal' control case — no severe single defect.
Manga full-body, legs short relative to torso. Proportion is the designed defect.
Young man leaning strongly off-axis. Balance/center-of-gravity is the teachable issue.
Anatomical male mid-stride walking. Gesture/weight-shift is the focus.
Chibi/SD — huge head, tiny body. The 'proportion defect' is a deliberate style. Flagging proportion here would be WRONG advice; ideal is to recognize stylization. Ambiguous by design.
Vibrant colored mage, dynamic. Designed for a color-focused message — should route to color when asked, not default to pose/proportion.
Realistic oil mountains with good aerial perspective. Control case — strong depth.
Ink line landscape, uniform line weight, no aerial gradation -> flat depth is the designed defect.
Filename says landscape but it's a still life (fruit bowl, bottle, cloth). Routing test: should treat as object/still-life, NOT force a landscape/perspective read.
